findings.evidence: `rg -n "@\[expose\]" code/TauCeti/Probability/Exchangeability` returns only the nine definitions at Basic.lean:39,44,49,54,59,64,70,75,81; characteristic lemmas already exist at Basic.lean:85-108 and 152-164 plus Contractability.lean:28-62.
findings.fix: Remove `@[expose]` from these definitions and add any missing named theorem needed by downstream code instead of making bodies reducible.
findings.issue: Every new definition body is exposed with `@[expose]`, including `blockLaw`, `prefixLaw`, `pathLaw`, `prefixProj`, `shift`, and the symmetry predicates, even though the file supplies simp/eliminator API for using them without unfolding.

findings.evidence: Basic.lean:33 declares arbitrary `{ι : Type*}` and Basic.lean:40 defines `blockLaw ... (k : ι → ℕ) : Measure (ι → α)`, while the roadmap target states finite laws over `Fin n` and a separate `pathLaw` at roadmap/TauCetiRoadmap/Exchangeability/Targets.lean:53-87.
findings.fix: Keep the arbitrary-index helper private, or narrow the public API to the finite selections actually used downstream, e.g. `Fin m → ℕ`, with separate named path-law lemmas for the `ℕ` case.
findings.issue: `blockLaw` is public over an arbitrary index type `ι`, so the public surface is larger than the Layer 0 finite-prefix/path-law target and overlaps `pathLaw` when `ι = ℕ`.

summary: The new exchangeability API still exposes implementation bodies and exports an over-general block-law primitive beyond the finite/path-law surface needed by the roadmap target.
